theory.intro: When adding or subtracting numbers with different numbers of digits, line them up by place value so each column holds ones, tens, hundreds and so on.
facts: **Align the place values** (ones under ones, tens under tens) — line up the right-hand ends for whole numbers.
Add a column at a time, **carrying** into the next column when it reaches $10$ or more.
When adding three or more numbers, a column total can be larger than $19$ — carry the full tens part.
steps: Write the numbers in a column, aligned by place value.
Add (or subtract) the ones, then tens, then hundreds, carrying or borrowing as needed.
